name: AZQ Stage 3 Wave D Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 3 Wave D so the Codex runner can evaluate the bounded pre-Stage-4 cleanup wave without hardcoding one exact refactor shape into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the Wave D cleanup.

baseline_checks.name: stage_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the current Stage 1 through Stage 3 regression suite still passes.

baseline_checks.name: agenda_and_scintilla_imports
baseline_checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.agenda.storage
import azq.agenda.build
import azq.agenda.dags
import azq.agenda.task_storage
import azq.scintilla.cli
import azq.scintilla.sparks
import azq.scintilla.spark_view
import azq.scintilla.spark_search
import azq.scintilla.spark_delete
PY
baseline_checks.description: Verify the current Agenda and Scintilla public modules still import cleanly.

STAGE3-WAVED-01.description: Stabilize Agenda task-lineage resolution behind one shared exact-id policy.

checks.name: agenda_lineage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the new Agenda lineage module exists.

checks.name: agenda_lineage_module_imports_and_has_resolution_surface
checks.description: Verify the new Agenda lineage module imports cleanly and exposes at least one public callable that looks like lineage or ancestry resolution.
checks.code:
import azq.agenda.lineage as lineage
public_callables = [name for name, value in vars(lineage).items() if callable(value) and not name.startswith('_')]
assert public_callables, 'no public callables exported from azq.agenda.lineage'
assert any(('resolve' in name.lower()) or ('lineage' in name.lower()) or ('ances' in name.lower()) for name in public_callables), public_callables

checks.name: agenda_touched_modules_import_cleanly_after_lineage_cleanup
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.agenda.lineage
import azq.agenda.build
import azq.agenda.task_storage
import azq.agenda.dags
import azq.agenda.storage
PY
checks.description: Verify the touched Agenda modules still import cleanly after the lineage cleanup.

checks.name: agenda_touched_modules_reference_shared_lineage_seam
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
files = [
    'azq/agenda/build.py',
    'azq/agenda/task_storage.py',
    'azq/agenda/dags.py',
    'azq/agenda/storage.py'
]
count = 0
for filename in files:
    text = Path(filename).read_text(encoding='utf-8').lower()
    if 'lineage' in text:
        count += 1
assert count >= 2, f'expected at least 2 touched Agenda modules to reference the lineage seam, found {count}'
PY
checks.description: Verify the new lineage seam is actually referenced by the touched Agenda modules instead of existing as an unused file.

human_checks: Lineage truth should now be clearly owned by one explicit Agenda seam rather than reconstructed separately in build, read, and DAG code paths.

The cleanup should preserve the live policy that deliverable_id remains the direct canonical parent backlink for tasks unless the implementation explicitly and coherently documents a different live policy.
Avoid replacing a small lineage seam with a broad shared repository abstraction or new Stage 4 reader behavior.

STAGE3-WAVED-02.description: Extract Scintilla storage and exact-id artifact helpers into a focused module.

checks.name: scintilla_storage_module_exists
checks.description: Verify the focused Scintilla storage module exists.

checks.name: scintilla_storage_imports_and_exposes_helper_surface
checks.description: Verify the new Scintilla storage module exposes canonical path ownership, exact-id artifact lookup, and stable listing helpers without overfitting to one exact function name.
checks.code:
import azq.scintilla.storage as storage
constant_groups = [
    ['AUDIO_DIR', 'SCINTILLA_AUDIO_DIR'],
    ['TRANSCRIPT_DIR', 'TRANSCRIPTS_DIR', 'SCINTILLA_TRANSCRIPT_DIR', 'SCINTILLA_TRANSCRIPTS_DIR'],
    ['SPARK_DIR', 'SPARKS_DIR', 'SCINTILLA_SPARK_DIR', 'SCINTILLA_SPARKS_DIR']
]
callable_groups = [
    ['ensure_scintilla_directories', 'ensure_directories'],
    ['spark_artifact_paths', 'get_spark_artifact_paths', 'resolve_spark_artifacts', 'resolve_spark_paths'],
    ['list_spark_files', 'list_sparks', 'iter_spark_files']
]
for group in constant_groups:
    assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in group), group
for group in callable_groups:
    assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in group), group

checks.name: scintilla_touched_modules_import_cleanly_after_storage_cleanup
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.scintilla.storage
import azq.scintilla.capture
import azq.scintilla.transcribe
import azq.scintilla.extract
import azq.scintilla.sparks
import azq.scintilla.spark_view
import azq.scintilla.spark_search
import azq.scintilla.spark_delete
PY
checks.description: Verify the touched Scintilla modules still import cleanly after helper extraction.

checks.name: scintilla_read_surface_modules_reference_shared_storage_seam
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
files = [
    'azq/scintilla/capture.py',
    'azq/scintilla/transcribe.py',
    'azq/scintilla/extract.py',
    'azq/scintilla/sparks.py',
    'azq/scintilla/spark_view.py',
    'azq/scintilla/spark_search.py',
    'azq/scintilla/spark_delete.py'
]
count = 0
for filename in files:
    text = Path(filename).read_text(encoding='utf-8').lower()
    if 'storage' in text:
        count += 1
assert count >= 3, f'expected at least 3 touched Scintilla modules to reference the shared storage seam, found {count}'
PY
checks.description: Verify the shared Scintilla storage seam is actually referenced by the touched modules instead of existing as an unused helper file.

human_checks: Path ownership and exact spark artifact resolution should now feel centralized rather than duplicated across list, view, search, and delete modules.
The current live Scintilla command surface should remain intact. This task should not quietly introduce archive or stewardship semantics.
If some duplication remains for pragmatic reasons, the shared helper seam should still own the main exact-id artifact bundle reconstruction.

STAGE3-WAVED-03.description: Align top-level CLI help and high-signal docs to the live Stage 3 surface.

checks.name: top_level_cli_help_teaches_live_stage3_surface
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'agenda build',
    'agenda list',
    'agenda show',
    'agenda dag',
    'goal add'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
assert 'Stage 2 command listing' not in text, 'stale Stage 2 help label still present'
PY
checks.description: Verify azq/cli.py help text reflects the live Stage 3 surface instead of an older Stage 2 snapshot.

checks.name: readme_and_bootstrap_teach_live_agenda_storage_and_commands
checks.description: Verify README.md and docs/architecture/AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md teach the live Agenda storage model and command family.

checks.name: architecture_docs_use_live_package_root_and_stage3_command_family
checks.description: Verify the architecture and planning docs teach the live package root and no longer teach stale src/azq layout wording.

checks.name: operator_facing_help_and_docs_do_not_present_stale_live_surface
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
files = ['azq/cli.py', 'README.md', 'docs/architecture/AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md']
forbidden = [
    'azq task start <task_id>',
    'azq task complete <task_id>',
    'azq dag build <goal_id>',
    'azq dag show <goal_id>',
    'azq spark show <id>',
    'src/azq/'
]
for filename in files:
    text = Path(filename).read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    for snippet in forbidden:
        assert snippet not in text, f'{filename}: stale live-surface snippet still present: {snippet}'
PY
checks.description: Verify the targeted operator-facing help and docs no longer present stale command families or stale package-root wording as the live surface.

human_checks: The doc updates should read like grounded operator guidance rather than a brittle find-and-replace sweep.
Future-stage ideas may remain in planning docs, but they should be clearly separated from the current live Stage 1 through Stage 3 surface.
If any historical command language remains for context, it should not be easy to confuse with live behavior.

STAGE3-WAVED-04.description: Add a compact Scintilla regression slice for shared read-surface behavior.

checks.name: stage3_wave_d_test_file_exists
checks.description: Verify the Stage 3 Wave D regression test file exists.

checks.name: stage3_wave_d_regression_suite_discovery_run
checks.description: Verify the repository's standard unittest discovery command still succeeds with the new Wave D tests present.

checks.name: stage3_wave_d_test_file_covers_scintilla_seams
checks.description: Verify the Wave D regression slice includes meaningful Scintilla seam coverage anchors without overfitting to one exact internal test style.

human_checks: The new tests should exercise the live read surface and exact-id behavior rather than merely asserting that helper functions exist.
If the delete path remains live, the tests should cover current delete behavior as preservation work, not as an endorsement of future destructive policy.
The suite should stay small and cleanup-focused rather than sprawling into microphone capture runtime, Whisper performance, or Stage 4 stewardship semantics.
